data.title: WTI crude turning lighter, risks losing favor with refiners - report
data.description:
Oil futures rose for a second consecutive session on Tuesday, as uncertainty in the Middle East outweighs supply and demand concerns for now, with analysts pointing to heightened sensitivity to an Israeli attack on Iran that appears to be imminent rather than delayed.

U.S. Secretary of State Blinken met Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu in the first big push for a Middle East ceasefire since Israel killed the leader of Hamas last week, but observers are skeptical after 11 previous visits to the region since the war in Gaza started made little progress towards a ceasefire.

But "assuming no supply disruptions in the Middle East, the oil balance looks increasingly comfortable through 2025," ING analysts Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson wrote. "With the market returning to a sizable surplus, we should at least see the front end of the curve moving into contango," referring to the market structure which indicates oversupply.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery closed +2.1% to $72.09/bbl, and front-month December Brent (CO1:COM) ended +2.3% to $76.04/bbl, the best settlement value for both benchmarks since October 14.

Front-month November Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) finished flat at $2.311/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (CRAK), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Companies drilling for crude in the U.S. are facing an unexpected dilemma: West Texas Midland crude is getting lighter, which could make it less appealing to some refiners.

Reuters reports shale producers in the Permian Basin have been pumping lighter crude as they exhaust first-tier production areas and move into second-tier acreage, with recent testing showing the oil's gravity, or measure of density, is at 41-44 degrees, compared to WTI Midland crude's historical gravity of 38-42 degrees; the higher the number, the lighter the oil.

Lighter crude generally is valued more than heavier crude, but refineries are set up for specific densities, usually not super-light crude, and they look for crude that can deliver the best margins from existing equipment; converting units to run lighter crudes economically would require spending on new gear.

data.title: Lilly Alzheimer's drug not to be covered by U.K.'s NHS: report
data.description:
U.K. regulators will reportedly recommend that the National Health Service not cover Eli Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) donanemab for the treatment of early Alzheimer’s disease because it’s too expensive.

According to The Telegraph, U.K. regulators are preparing to rule that donanemab is safe. That decision, however, will be followed by a recommendation from the NHS’s rationing body, the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence, or NICE, that the treatment is too costly to cover.

The U.K. newspaper noted that the estimated annual cost of donanemab in the U.S. is $32,000, which is around 25% higher than Esai’s (OTCPK:ESAIY) Alzheimer's drug lecanemab. Including scans and monitoring, treatment with donanemab costs an average of $78,000 per year in the U.S., it added.

In August, NICE issued a draft recommendation that the NHS not cover lecanemab because its benefits were too small to justify the cost.

The FDA approved donanemab in July. Lilly markets the product in the U.S. under the name Kisunla.

The agency granted accelerated approval to lecanemab in January 2023. The product is marketed by Esai (OTCPK:ESAIY) and Biogen (BIIB) in the U.S. under the name Leqembi.

data.title: Eli Lilly Alzheimer's med donanemab likely to face UK NHS rejection
data.description:
• The UK's National Health Service is likely to rule Oct. 23 that Eli Lilly's (NYSE:LLY) Alzheimer's disease therapy donanemab, while safe, will not be approved for use due to its high cost.
  • The NHS decision is expected to follow in the footsteps of one made earlier this year on Biogen's (BIIB) lecanemab, The Telegraph reported. In August, draft guidance issued by the National Institute for Health and Care Excellence, which sets drug policy for NHS, said that treatment was too pricey to justify coverage.
  • The Telegraph, just days after the lecanemab draft guidance came out, predicted that donanemab would face the same fate, citing health sources.
  • In the U.S., donanemab treatment costs ~$78,000 per year when necessary scans and monitoring are factored in.

data.title: Pfizer RSV vaccine Abrysvo gains expanded indication
data.description:
• The U.S. FDA has approved Pfizer's respiratory syncytial virus vaccine Abrysvo for individuals 18 to 59 years old that are at increased risk of lower respiratory tract disease from the virus.
  • Those conditions include obesity, diabetes, chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, heart failure, chronic kidney disease, and asthma.
  • Expanded approval was based on results of the phase 3 MONeT study, which examined the shot in adults at risk of RSV-associated disease due to certain chronic medical conditions.

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles rose 1.6M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a build of 1.64M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending October 18, after API reported a 1.6M-barrel draw a week earlier.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a draw of 2.02M barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a draw of 1.48M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing, Oklahoma, storage hub show a draw of 216K barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report Wednesday morning; analysts surveyed by The Wall Street Journal forecasts commercial crude stocks will increase by 800K barrels, gasoline inventories will decline by 1.3M barrels, and distillates will decline by 1.6M barrels.

November WTI crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $71.37/bbl after settling Wednesday at $72.09/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)

data.title: Fokus Mining appoints Philippe MacKay as CEO
data.description:
• Fokus Mining (TSXV:FKM:CA) on Tuesday has appointed Philippe MacKay as chief executive officer, effective November 4.
  • Additionaly, MacKay and Kerry Sparkes have also been appointed as directors, the company said.
  • MacKay joined Fokus after nearly ten years at the Osisko Group, where he last held the position of Mine Manager for the Cariboo Gold Project.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Encompass Health issues notice for partial redemption of 5.75% senior notes due 2025
data.description:
• Encompass Health (NYSE:EHC) on Tuesday issued notice for redemption of $100 million of the outstanding principal balance of its 5.75% senior notes due 2025.
  • The associated redemption date will be November 21, and the redemption price will be 100% of par, plus accrued and unpaid interest pursuant to the terms of the 2025 notes.
  • The company plans to use cash on hand to fund the redemption. As a result of this redemption, it expects to record an about $0.2 million loss on early extinguishment of debt in the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Enphase Energy plunges after wide Q3 miss, downbeat Q4 guidance
data.description:
Enphase Energy (NASDAQ:ENPH) -8.8% post-market Tuesday after reporting a wide miss on Q3 adjusted earnings and a 31% Y/Y decline in revenues, while also guiding Q4 revenues well below Wall Street consensus.

Q3 net income fell to $45.76M, or $0.33/share, from $113.95M, or $0.80/share, in the year-earlier quarter; GAAP gross margin fell to 46.8% from 47.5% a year ago, and non-GAAP gross margin fell to 48.1%, including net IRA benefit of 9.2%, from 48.4% a year ago.

For Q4, Enphase (ENPH) issued downside guidance for Q4 revenues of $360M-$400M vs. $433.5M analyst consensus, with non-GAAP gross margin seen in a range of 49%-52% with net IRA benefit and 39%-42% excluding net IRA benefit.

Enphase's (ENPH) results appear to be weighing on solar peers after-hours, including SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) -3.7%, Sunrun (RUN) -2.2%, Sunnova Energy (NOVA) -1.7%, First Solar (FSLR) -0.7%.

data.title: Li Bang International announces pricing of initial public offering
data.description:
• Li Bang International Corporation (NASDAQ:LBGJ) on Tuesday announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 1.52M ordinary shares at a public offering price of $4.00 per ordinary share.
  • The ordinary shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq and are expected to commence trading on October 23, under the ticker symbol “LBGJ.”
  • The company expects to receive aggregate gross proceeds of $6.08 million from the offering.
  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 228,000 ordinary shares, representing 15% of the ordinary shares sold in the offering.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 24.
  • Net proceeds from the offering will be used for plant construction, equipment procurement, advertising and marketing, research and development, and general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SAG Holdings announces pricing of its IPO
data.description:
• SAG Holdings (NASDAQ:SAG) announced the pricing of its initial public offering of 875,000 ordinary shares at a price of $8.00 per share to the public, for a total of US$7M of gross proceeds.
  • The ordinary shares will begin trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on October 23, 2024, under the symbol “SAG.”
  • Source: Press Release
